stories.headline: Aave V3 USDC pool stuck at 99.87% utilization with under $3M left as Circle pushes urgent fix
stories.tldr: Aave's flagship USDC market is effectively drained, leaving lenders unable to withdraw and triggering Circle to demand emergency rate action.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A frozen USDC market on the largest DeFi lender risks cascading liquidations and signals that post-Kelp deposit flight has now broken Aave's core money market, not just its riskier pools.
the_details: Aave V3's USDC pool has held at 99.87% utilization for days with less than $3M of available liquidity for withdrawals.
Over $8.4B in deposits left Aave within 48 hours after the Kelp and Drift exploits, hollowing out reserves.
Circle is pressing the Aave DAO to hike rates or adjust caps to restore borrow/lend balance before forced liquidations cascade.

stories.headline: Strategy scoops another 3,273 BTC for $255M, treasury balloons to 818,334 BTC
stories.tldr: Michael Saylor's Strategy disclosed a $255M bitcoin purchase, lifting the corporate treasury to a record 818,334 BTC at an avg price near $77,900.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Strategy is buying directly into a sub-$80K market, signaling continued conviction even as spot price stalls and giving bulls a concrete bid to point at while ETFs absorb supply.
the_details: Strategy bought 3,273 BTC for ~$255M on April 27, implying an average price near $77,900.
Total holdings now sit at 818,334 BTC, the largest corporate bitcoin treasury on record.
Disclosure landed alongside BlackRock's IBIT logging its biggest inflow day in six months.

stories.headline: BlackRock's IBIT logs $983M inflow day, biggest in six months as GBTC sheds $54M
stories.tldr: Spot Bitcoin ETFs saw a sharp rotation Monday, with IBIT pulling $983M in fresh capital while Grayscale's GBTC and Bitwise's BITB posted outflows.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A near-$1B single-day haul into IBIT during a price stall confirms institutional bid is still scaling, even as retail-led products bleed — a pattern that has historically preceded leg-up moves.
the_details: iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) recorded $983M in net inflows, its highest one-day total in six months.
Grayscale's GBTC bled $54.18M and Bitwise's BITB shed $11.35M on the same session.
Inflows hit as BTC traded between $77,700 and $79,000 with $80K acting as a hard ceiling.

stories.headline: BTC slams into $80K wall as bitcoin-dollar inverse correlation hits 4-year extreme
stories.tldr: Bitcoin briefly cracked $79K before sellers capped the move below $80K, with BTC and DXY now moving in near-perfect opposition for the first time since 2022.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A clean inverse with the dollar reframes BTC as a macro hedge rather than a risk asset this cycle, making upcoming FOMC and Iran-talks headlines the dominant short-term catalysts.
the_details: BTC opened at $78,670, hit $79,000+ in Asia, then slipped back to ~$77,700 by 9am ET.
Analysts flagged concentrated sell orders at $80K despite record IBIT inflows and $5B in fresh USDT supply.
BTC-USD correlation with DXY is the most negative in nearly four years, per CoinDesk's daybook.

stories.headline: SEC carves out broker-dealer exemption for DEX front-ends, clearing path for compliant DeFi UIs
stories.tldr: An SEC staff statement says 'Covered User Interface Providers' running non-custodial DEX front-ends won't need broker-dealer registration, easing a key DeFi overhang.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: This is the first concrete olive branch from the SEC to DeFi front-end operators in years, narrowing the legal gray zone that drove projects offshore and unlocking US-hosted interfaces.
the_details: April 13 SEC statement exempts non-custodial UI providers for crypto-asset securities from broker-dealer registration.
Statement is paired with the joint SEC-CFTC interpretive release from March that set a 5-category token taxonomy.
Over 100 crypto firms including Coinbase and Ripple are pressing the Senate to mark up the Clarity Act this session.
